title: Informatics Research on Mental Health Functioning — Decision Support for the SSA Disability Program (Goldman et al., 2022)
description: Peer-reviewed article (Goldman HH et al., Psychiatric Services, 2022) describing informatics and natural-language-processing research aimed at extracting mental-health functioning information from disability case records to support SSA disability decision-making. Examines how structured functional data could improve consistency of determinations involving mental impairments. Relevant to DDS efforts to apply NLP/decision-support tooling to disability adjudication.

source.publisher: National Center for Biotechnology Information (NCBI) PubMed Central (PMC); published in Psychiatric Services
source.title: Informatics Research on Mental Health Functioning – Decision Support for the SSA Disability Program (Psychiatr Serv. 2022 Jun 2;74(1):56-62; doi:10.1176/appi.ps.202200056)

notes: Authoritative: peer-reviewed journal article (Psychiatric Services) mirrored as an NIH public-access author manuscript from PMC (PMC10501504; DOI 10.1176/appi.ps.202200056). Published in final edited form 2022 Jun 2; available in PMC 2024 Jan 1. Single publication year so temporal_coverage start==end (2022). Markdown scrape retains PMC page chrome and author/reference links; no tabular columns to declare.
